title: memstead-plugin/v1 .memstead.toml
description: Top-level workspace manifest for a memstead-plugin/v1 workspace. The plugin reads this file to discover the registered mems and any client- or plugin-namespaced configuration. The engine consumes only `mems`; the four-primitive configs live at fixed `.memstead/{mediums,facets,projections}/` paths rather than via directory-pointer keys (the retired `ingests/` dir and its `ingest` primitive are gone in v1 — a binding carries its operations inline). The remainder is plugin-owned. Validation gates the plugin loader on the `format` version key. Pass-through tables under `[clients.*]` and `[plugin.*]` are accepted without further constraint. The retired `schemas_dir` key is undocumented in v1 (workspace schemas load from the fixed `.memstead/schemas/` path).

format.description: Pinned format version. Producers must emit this exact string for v1; the plugin loader rejects any other value with a clear error listing supported versions.

mems.description: Explicitly registered mem paths, relative to this file's directory. No auto-discovery. Runtime-created mems are appended here by `memstead_mem_create`.

mutations.description: Engine-owned mutation policy. See WorkspaceToml in memstead-schema for accepted keys.

mem_management.description: Engine-owned mem lifecycle policy. Two array-of-tables — `[[mem_management.create]]` (per rule: `pattern` + `schemas[]`) and `[[mem_management.delete]]` (per rule: `pattern` only). Glob matching is gitignore-style over the composed candidate `<path>/<name>`.

drift.description: Engine-owned disk-drift policy. See WorkspaceToml. A v1 workspace never carries a `realizationPatterns` sub-key — provenance from source artifact → entity lives in engine-owned anchors, not schema regexes.

clients.description: Pass-through namespace for client-specific configuration tables (e.g. `[clients.claude_code]`). Keys and shapes are owned by individual clients; the plugin loader does not constrain them.

plugin.description: Pass-through namespace for plugin-specific configuration tables (e.g. `[plugin.claude_code.outer_vcs]`). Keys and shapes are owned by individual plugins; the loader does not constrain them.
